grain: one row per award notice (BUYER-SIDE; NO winner dimension)
trustworthy_metrics: COUNT of notices + MEDIAN value + procedure-type mix; never the naive sum
next_step: sql_views/ted_buyer_*.sql for authority-activity + procedure-type trends; winner backfill is a separate per-notice-XML build (doc/TED_ENRICHMENT.md §3.5)

source.dataset: TED — Tenders Electronic Daily (award-notice activity, buyer-side, Ireland)
source.publisher: Publications Office of the European Union
source.license: EU open data — reuse authorised under Commission Decision 2011/833/EU
source.attribution: Contains information from TED (© European Union), reused under Decision 2011/833/EU.
source.winner_caveat: BUYER-SIDE ONLY. The winner is NOT in this layer: the API returns 0% winner fields for pre-2024 legacy notices. Use ted_ie_awards.parquet (2024+) for supplier-side analysis; never join this layer to suppliers.

caveat: BUYER-SIDE award-notice activity. One row per notice; the WINNER is NOT present (API returns 0% winner fields for pre-2024 legacy notices). total-value is the notice's total awarded value; framework ceilings cannot be separated without a winner count, so framework detection is value-threshold-only and value_safe_to_sum is conservative. Lead with COUNT. Never merge with or sum against the winner silver or eTenders.
